withheldFacts.reason: Institution slide attributes this to Health Workforce Australia, abolished 2014. Withheld pending verification against current Department of Health workforce modelling. Do not narrate until cleared at review gate 1.

verificationFlags.note: Master slides cite QS 2024 by Subject; the institution's own study guide separately claims 'top 50 for nursing' citing QS 2025. Year conflict in source material — institution must confirm which edition to teach at review gate 1. Card below carries the conservative label.

_pipelineNotes.assessmentGate: Level 3 badge is gated on the AI roleplay consultation (scenario ref: rp-health-amara-01), not the MCQ bank. Retrieve questions feed the adaptive scheduler only.

narration.text: When a student asks whether La Trobe is genuinely strong in health, you need evidence, not adjectives.
Start with nursing. La Trobe is ranked forty-second in the world for nursing in the QS subject rankings.
The wider health portfolio holds up too: top one hundred and seventy-five globally for medical and health, and top one hundred and fifty for public health.
One professional habit: always quote the ranking with its source and year. A ranking without provenance is a compliance risk, not a selling point.

props.headline: 42nd
props.sourceLabel: QS 2024 by Subject — confirm current edition

props.message: Quote rankings with source and year. Misquoted or stale rankings in counselling material are a compliance risk.

props.text: Evidence, not adjectives: every ranking you quote carries its source and its year.

narration.text: Rankings open the conversation. Placements close it.
La Trobe health students train through three settings: on-site clinical placements, the university's own public clinics run under expert clinicians, and Australia's largest Rural Health School.
For a student weighing offers, this is the differentiator: patient contact before graduation, not after it.

props.overlayText: Clinical training, on campus and in community

props.text: The placement story is the differentiator: patient contact before graduation.

narration.text: Five postgraduate courses do most of the work in this portfolio, and you should be able to place each one without looking it up.

The Master of Digital Health, for technology-focused careers. Health Information Management and Health Administration, for the systems side. Counselling, Rehabilitation and Mental Health, for allied practice. And the Master of Public Health, for population-level work.
Each attracts a different student profile, and mismatching them is the most common health counselling error.

items.text: Master of Counselling, Rehabilitation & Mental Health

props.text: Five flagship masters, five different student profiles — know which is which cold.

narration.text: Here is the distinction counsellors get wrong more than any other in health.

Some courses lead toward eligibility for professional registration, like nursing, midwifery, and occupational therapy. Others, like public health and health information management, build expertise but do not qualify a graduate to practise clinically.
Telling a student that a non-registration course leads to clinical practice is not a small mistake. It misrepresents their career pathway, their visa narrative, and their investment.

props.myth: A health masters qualifies you to practise clinically
props.fact: Only registration-track courses lead toward professional registration eligibility

props.message: Never state or imply that a non-registration course leads to clinical practice. Registration eligibility also involves professional bodies and English standards beyond admission.

props.text: Registration-track or not — settle this before any other health counselling question.

narration.text: Meet Amara. Twenty-four, from Lagos, a biology degree, two years in a hospital records office, and a goal of working in health systems, not at the bedside.
Her background points away from the registration-track courses and toward Health Information Management or Digital Health.
That is the matching method: background, career goal, registration need — in that order. The course recommendation falls out of the answers.

props.text: Background → goal → registration need. The course match falls out of the answers.

narration.text: Facilities are facts a student can verify, which makes them ideal material for a genuine student narrative.
Three to know. An eighty-two million dollar clinical teaching building at Bundoora, adding capacity to train four hundred more allied health professionals a year.

A new clinical simulation suite at Albury-Wodonga, doubling regional training capacity. And La Trobe Private Hospital, a thirty-four bed teaching hospital run with Healthscope, offering placements for nursing, allied health, and health information students.

props.headline: A$82M
props.supporting: clinical teaching building, Bundoora — +400 allied health graduates per year

items.text: La Trobe Private Hospital — 34 beds, with Healthscope
Placements: nursing, allied health, health information

props.text: Facilities are verifiable facts — the best raw material for a specific GS narrative.

narration.text: For research-minded students, La Trobe's health story runs through artificial intelligence and biotechnology.

ACAMI, the Australian Centre for Artificial Intelligence in Medical Innovation, is described by the university as the world's first university-led centre for AI-powered medical breakthroughs, partnered with mRNA Victoria.
The campus hosts an mRNA manufacturing facility in development with BioNTech, and La Trobe was the first Australian university to deploy NVIDIA's D-G-X H-two-hundred systems for medical research.
Notice the framing: these are the university's own claims, so present them as such — 'La Trobe describes ACAMI as the world's first' travels safely; a bare superlative does not.

items.text: ACAMI — AI in medical innovation, with mRNA Victoria
First Australian university with NVIDIA DGX H200

props.message: Superlatives ('world's first', 'Australia's largest') are institution claims. Attribute them — don't assert them as independent fact.

props.text: Attribute superlatives to the institution — 'La Trobe describes it as…' travels safely.

narration.text: The digital health investment thread ties the portfolio together.

La Trobe leads the Care Economy Cooperative Research Centre with a thirty-five million dollar federal funding boost and sixty partner organisations across care technology, data, and workforce innovation.

It also delivers the Victorian Virtual Emergency Department with Northern Health and Cisco — augmented reality that lets a remote doctor see what the on-site nurse sees, now permanent and available around the clock.
For a Digital Health applicant, these are exactly the named, checkable programs a strong genuine student statement is built from.

props.headline: A$35M
props.supporting: federal funding — Care Economy CRC, 60 partner organisations

items.text: Victorian Virtual ED — with Northern Health & Cisco
AR link: remote doctor sees what the nurse sees

props.text: Named, checkable programs beat generic praise in a GS statement — every time.

narration.text: One graduate journey shows the pattern you are selling.
Randi completed the Master of Digital Health, then a thirteen-month internship with Medibank, mentored by Medibank's Head of Virtual Health.
She researched Australian consumer behaviour in digital health, and moved into full-time work with a start-up testing AI accuracy in clinical settings.
Degree, embedded industry experience, employment. When you tell this story, tell it as a pattern the university enables — not a guarantee every student receives.

props.quote: La Trobe's industry work experience opportunities are really good pathways for students to develop their employability skills along with their studies.
props.attribution: Randi, Master of Digital Health graduate

props.heading: The pattern
props.note: A pattern the university enables — not a promise

props.text: Sell the pattern, never the promise: degree → embedded industry experience → employment.

narration.text: Regional campuses carry the health portfolio's strongest placement story.
Bendigo and Albury-Wodonga host the Rural Medical Pathway Program, and regional health students place into local hospitals and clinics where the workforce need is real.
Regional study can also carry post-study work advantages under the graduate visa settings — but here is the hard line.
You may state current, factual visa settings. You may never promise migration outcomes, permanent residency, or visa grants. That line is where counselling ends and misconduct begins.

props.message: State current visa settings. NEVER promise migration outcomes, PR, or visa grants. This is the misconduct line.

props.text: Facts about visa settings: yes. Promises about visa outcomes: never.

assessment.description: Counsellor conducts a simulated consultation with an AI student persona seeking health study advice. Rubric: registration-track distinction handled correctly, rankings quoted with provenance, placement story told as pattern not promise, no migration-outcome promises, appropriate course match with reasoning.

questionBank.prompt: A student asks: 'Is La Trobe actually good for nursing, or just easier to get into?' Your strongest opening answer is:
options: It's a top university in Australia
It's ranked 42nd in the world for nursing — QS subject rankings
Thousands of international students choose it
Its nursing degree is well respected
questionBank.explanation: Specific ranking + source beats generic praise. Always attach the source and year.

questionBank.prompt: Which source ranks La Trobe 42nd globally for nursing?
questionBank.explanation: QS World University Rankings by Subject. THE and CSIC underpin the Medical & Health top-175 claim; ShanghaiRanking underpins Public Health.

questionBank.prompt: Why must rankings always be quoted with source and year?
options: Different rankings measure different things and editions change — a stale or mismatched ranking is a compliance risk
questionBank.explanation: Rankings shift between editions and sources measure different things. Provenance protects the student and the counsellor.

questionBank.prompt: Where do La Trobe health students get supervised patient contact before graduating?
options: Only after graduation, in their first job
Through placements, university public clinics, and the Rural Health School
questionBank.explanation: Three settings: on-site clinical placements, La Trobe's public clinics under expert clinicians, and Australia's largest Rural Health School.

questionBank.prompt: La Trobe describes its Rural Health School as:
questionBank.explanation: 'Australia's largest Rural Health School' — an institution claim, so attribute it to La Trobe when you use it.

questionBank.prompt: The strongest placement-based differentiator to give a student comparing offers is:
options: Patient contact before graduation through embedded clinical placements
questionBank.explanation: Pre-graduation patient contact is the employability differentiator the placement model delivers.

questionBank.prompt: A student wants a health career 'in systems and data, not with patients'. Which course family do you reach for first?
questionBank.explanation: Systems-and-data goals point to HIM and Digital Health, not clinical registration tracks.

questionBank.prompt: Which of these is NOT one of the flagship postgraduate health courses covered in this module?
questionBank.explanation: The flagship five: Digital Health, Health Information Management, Health Administration, Counselling/Rehabilitation & Mental Health, and Public Health.

questionBank.prompt: A student with a psychology background wanting hands-on allied practice best matches:
options: Master of Counselling, Rehabilitation and Mental Health
questionBank.explanation: Counselling/Rehab & Mental Health is the allied-practice route among the five.

questionBank.prompt: True or false: completing a Master of Public Health qualifies a graduate to practise as a registered health professional.
questionBank.explanation: Public health is a non-registration course. It builds expertise but does not lead to clinical registration.

questionBank.prompt: Which group below is registration-track?
questionBank.explanation: Nursing, midwifery and OT lead toward professional registration eligibility; the systems courses do not.

questionBank.prompt: Why is misstating the registration distinction a serious error rather than a small one?
options: It misrepresents the student's career pathway, visa narrative, and financial investment
questionBank.explanation: It corrupts the three things counselling exists to protect: pathway, narrative, investment.

questionBank.prompt: Registration eligibility for clinical courses depends on:
options: Course completion plus professional-body requirements, including English standards that can exceed admission levels
questionBank.explanation: Professional bodies set their own requirements beyond university admission — flag this early with clinical aspirants.

questionBank.prompt: Amara: biology degree, 2 years in hospital records, wants health systems work, not bedside care. First course family to explore:
options: Health Information Management or Digital Health
questionBank.explanation: Background + systems goal + no registration need → HIM/Digital Health.

questionBank.prompt: The matching method, in order:
questionBank.explanation: Background, then goal, then registration need. The course match falls out of the answers.

questionBank.prompt: A student insists on nursing but their goal is hospital data management. You should:
options: Enrol them in nursing as requested
Explore the mismatch: explain the registration distinction and surface HIM/Digital Health before they commit
questionBank.explanation: Fit-based counselling means surfacing the mismatch, not rubber-stamping the request — the behaviour the credential certifies.

questionBank.prompt: Why do named facilities make strong GS-statement material?
options: They are specific, verifiable facts a case officer can check
They are required in the visa form
questionBank.explanation: GS assessment favours specific, checkable claims over generic praise.

questionBank.prompt: The A$82M clinical teaching building at Bundoora adds capacity for approximately:
questionBank.explanation: Around 400 additional allied health professionals per year — an institution claim from La Trobe's materials.

questionBank.prompt: La Trobe Private Hospital is:
options: A 34-bed hospital run with Healthscope offering student placements
questionBank.explanation: 34 beds, partnered with Healthscope, with placements for nursing, allied health and health information students.

questionBank.prompt: How should you present La Trobe's claim that ACAMI is 'the world's first university-led AI medical innovation centre'?
options: Attributed: 'La Trobe describes ACAMI as the world's first…'
questionBank.explanation: Superlatives are institution claims. Attribution keeps the statement safe and honest.

questionBank.prompt: The on-campus mRNA manufacturing facility is being developed with:
questionBank.explanation: BioNTech — connected to La Trobe's research in cancer, cardiovascular and infectious disease.

questionBank.prompt: La Trobe states it was the first Australian university to deploy which system for medical research?
questionBank.explanation: NVIDIA DGX H200 — supporting ACAMI's AI-driven medical research. An institution claim; attribute it.

questionBank.prompt: A Digital Health applicant needs named programs for their GS statement. Which pair is strongest?
options: Care Economy CRC and the Victorian Virtual Emergency Department
Campus food court and sports park
questionBank.explanation: Named, checkable programs — a CRC with federal funding and a live virtual ED — are exactly what a specific GS narrative is built from.

questionBank.prompt: The Care Economy CRC that La Trobe leads involves:
options: A$35M federal funding and 60 partner organisations
questionBank.explanation: A$35M federal boost, 60 partner organisations across care technology, data solutions and workforce innovation.

questionBank.prompt: The Victorian Virtual Emergency Department uses AR to:
options: Let a remote doctor see what the on-site nurse sees
questionBank.explanation: Delivered with Northern Health and Cisco — remote doctor sees the on-site nurse's view; now permanent and 24/7.

questionBank.prompt: When telling a graduate success story like Randi's, the correct framing is:
options: A guarantee of what the student will receive
A pattern the university enables, with the specifics named
A typical outcome for all graduates
Confidential information not to be shared
questionBank.explanation: Sell the pattern with named specifics; promising the outcome misleads the student.

questionBank.prompt: Randi's pathway ran:
options: Master of Public Health → hospital job
Master of Digital Health → 13-month Medibank internship → full-time digital health role
questionBank.explanation: Digital Health masters, 13-month Medibank internship with senior mentorship, then full-time work in clinical AI.

questionBank.prompt: What makes the Randi story usable in counselling (rather than just marketing)?
options: Every element is specific and attributable: named degree, named employer, named duration
questionBank.explanation: Specific, attributable elements can be verified and framed honestly as an enabled pattern.

questionBank.prompt: A student asks: 'If I study nursing at Bendigo, will I get PR?' Your answer:
options: 'Regional study can carry post-study work advantages under current settings — but no one can promise migration outcomes, and I won't'
questionBank.explanation: State current settings factually; never promise outcomes. This is the misconduct line.

questionBank.prompt: The Rural Medical Pathway Program runs at which campuses?
questionBank.explanation: Bendigo and Albury-Wodonga — per La Trobe's materials.

questionBank.prompt: Which statement is a counsellor permitted to make?
options: 'Regional graduates always get extended work rights'
'Under current settings, regional study can carry post-study work advantages — settings change, so we verify at application time'
questionBank.explanation: Factual, current, hedged for change, no outcome promise — the only compliant formulation of the four.
